bugfix_incident_log.bug_symptom: Two notes could use the same example text even when they belonged to different concepts, making review cards harder to distinguish.
bugfix_incident_log.fix: Track used examples and prefix a duplicate example with the card concept.
follow_up: If this becomes a real app
add user-facing duplicate warnings or richer example editing.
bugfix_incident_log.root_cause: The desired behavior needed explicit uniqueness tracking during card generation.
bugfix_incident_log.summary: Prevented duplicate example text from appearing across generated study cards.
bugfix_incident_log.verification: The scratch test `creates study cards and avoids duplicate examples across themes` passed after implementation.

current_idea:
Reading Card Lite is a small local Node.js utility that takes reading notes with theme, concept, explanation, and example fields, then produces grouped Markdown review cards. The prototype is intentionally tiny: no UI, no database, no account system, and no cloud sync.

Its purpose is to test whether VibeLog can record an agent-led vibe coding process with test design, idea change, human decision, exact execution prompt, development work, incident thinking, verification evidence, artifact boundaries, and handoff state.

decisions.decision: Keep Reading Card Lite source code outside the VibeLog repository.
decisions.impact: The example directory contains only `README.md`, `vibe-log.md`, and `vibe-log.json`.
decisions.why: This repository is skill-first. Examples should show generated VibeLog records, not turn the skill repository into an application source repository.

decisions.decision: Use automated agent dogfood verification instead of asking the human to manually inspect every step.
decisions.impact: Slice 4 focuses on repeatable commands, example integrity tests, exporter checks, validator checks, and concise evidence reporting.
decisions.why: The user's principle is that anything an agent can vibe-verify should not become human manual work.

development_log.details: Tests cover normalized theme grouping, study card creation, duplicate example handling, and grouped Markdown export.
development_log.summary: Added scratch tests for Reading Card Lite behavior.

development_log.details: Added theme grouping, normalized note handling, study card generation, duplicate example prefixing, review question generation, and grouped Markdown export.
development_log.follow_up: Use generated VibeLog records as the repository example.
development_log.summary: Implemented Reading Card Lite scratch logic.

development_log.details: The example documents the scratch project process while keeping source code outside the repository.
development_log.follow_up: Export JSON and run integrity checks.

execution_prompts.prompt_summary: Retry and execute Slice 4 dogfood verification locally without pushing.
execution_prompts.result: Codex resumed Slice 4, wrote red example-integrity tests, created guides, ran a scratch Reading Card Lite project with TDD, and generated this VibeLog example.
execution_prompts.reuse_notes: A short human trigger can still be a build prompt when it resumes an approved implementation plan.

execution_prompts.prompt_summary: Execute the plan-defined scratch Reading Card Lite dogfood task.
execution_prompts.prompt_text: Create a scratch Reading Card Lite project outside this repository, write tests first, confirm RED, implement the minimal Node.js logic, confirm GREEN, then copy only generated VibeLog records into `examples/reading-card-lite/`.
execution_prompts.result: Scratch tests passed and generated VibeLog records were created.
execution_prompts.reuse_notes: This is a reusable agent dogfood prompt pattern for future examples.

completed: Scratch folder created outside the repository.
GREEN run observed with 3 passing tests.

This example validates process recording, not a production reading-card product.
handoff_state.current_state: Reading Card Lite exists as a local scratch project used to verify VibeLog dogfood behavior. The VibeLog repository contains only generated example records.

human_in_the_loop.final_decision: The dogfood run should be agent-verified first.

idea_evolution.after: Create Reading Card Lite as a scratch project outside the repository to verify VibeLog with a small but real coding task.
idea_evolution.before: Slice 4 needed agent-run dogfood verification, but no real unfinished project was available.
idea_evolution.reason: The project needed proof that VibeLog can be used without asking the human to manually prepare a project.

idea_evolution.after: Preserve input order for generated cards while sorting grouped Markdown output by theme.
idea_evolution.reason: This makes duplicate example handling easier to verify in tests while keeping the exported review material organized.

assumptions: notes are already extracted; each note has a theme
idea_expansion.core_value: Turn reading notes into structured recall material.
idea_expansion.product_hypothesis: A small transformation core is enough to prove the review-card idea before any UI exists.
use_cases: hand off notes to another study tool.
why_it_matters: The project has real enough behavior to test implementation
and handoff without bloating the VibeLog repository.

completed: Confirmed GREEN with 3 passing scratch tests.
implementation_status.current_state: Reading Card Lite is a local scratch project with tested note grouping, card creation, duplicate example handling, and Markdown export logic. This repository contains only the generated VibeLog example.
important_context_for_next_agent: Do not copy scratch `src/`, `test/`, or `package.json` into this repository.
The scratch app is evidence for VibeLog verification, not a product being shipped here.
The important reusable artifact is the process record.
next_actions: Run drift check and full repository tests.

one_line_vibe: A tiny study-card generator that turns reading notes into grouped review cards.
open_questions: Should future examples be fully bilingual or use English content plus Chinese README notes?
Should the next slice implement automatic hook-style recording before adding more examples?
Should scratch project paths be redacted in future public exports?

do_not_change: Do not add scratch source files to the VibeLog repository.
Do not present Reading Card Lite as a shipped product.
important_files: `examples/reading-card-lite/vibe-log.md`: generated VibeLog record in this repository.
`examples/reading-card-lite/vibe-log.json`: generated JSON export in this repository.
known_issues: This is a scratch utility, not a production reading app.
The example does not include screenshots because the scratch project has no UI.

public_private_projection.public_summary: Reading Card Lite is a scratch dogfood example showing how VibeLog records an agent-run mini project from tests to handoff.

public_summary: Reading Card Lite is a scratch dogfood example showing that VibeLog can record an agent-run mini project from idea through test design, RED/GREEN implementation, incident thinking, verification evidence, artifact boundaries, and handoff. The source code stays outside the VibeLog skill repository; only generated VibeLog records are included here.
title: Reading Card Lite

automated_test_strategy: Use `npm test` in the scratch project.
Use `node --test test/vibelog-examples.test.mjs` for repository-boundary and example-integrity checks.
Use `node --test` for the full repository test suite.
core_user_paths: Generate study cards with review questions.
Avoid duplicate example text across cards.
edge_cases: Missing future UI or storage should not affect core record validity.
manual_test_steps: Confirm the log explains the idea, status, evidence, and handoff.
Confirm the example directory contains generated VibeLog records only.
regression_points: Do not add scratch source code to `examples/reading-card-lite/`.
Do not drop required VibeLog sections from generated examples.
risks_safety_privacy_checks: No real personal reading notes are used.
The scratch source path is local-only.
The example is private by default until the human chooses a publication policy.
success_criteria: Agent can run a scratch project without human manual setup.
Scratch tests prove behavior before and after implementation.
VibeLog records the idea, changes, human decisions, exact engineering prompt, development work, incident thinking, validation, verification, artifacts, and handoff.
Example Markdown and JSON stay synchronized.
Example directory contains generated records only.

verification_evidence.residual_risk: This proves the tests caught missing implementation, not final behavior.
verification_evidence.summary: Watched scratch tests fail before implementation because `src/reading-card-lite.mjs` did not exist.

verification_evidence.details: 3 tests passed: grouping notes by normalized theme, creating cards without duplicate examples, and exporting grouped Markdown cards.
verification_evidence.residual_risk: The scratch product is intentionally small and does not cover UI, storage, sync, or AI note extraction.
verification_evidence.summary: Scratch tests passed after implementing the reading-card logic.

vibe_intake.desired_outcome: Convert notes into grouped review cards that are easy to scan.
vibe_intake.problem: Reading notes often remain scattered and are hard to review later.

vibe_progress.next: Export JSON, validate example, run integrity tests, update root VibeLog, and write Slice 4 report.
vibe_progress.problems: Needed a repeatable verification scenario without asking the human to manually supply a new unfinished project.
vibe_progress.what_happened: Ran Reading Card Lite as a scratch agent dogfood project to verify VibeLog recording.
